learning_outcomes.description: Define a problem, state an hypothesis and propose a process for the investigation of the problem.
Design and implement a research project, analyse and interpret data, discuss results and draw relevant conclusions.
Produce a research report in the form of a potential journal article.
Deliver an oral presentation on a defined topic.
Demonstrate professional behaviour in the context of the researchable topic, in consultation with the supervisory team.

assessments.task_description:
Component 1 - Research Proposal (20%)

A written assessment whose aim is to provide students with the tools to more effectively manage their project as well as allow the opportunity for a personal reflection. This will be marked by your principal supervisor.

The purpose of this 4-part mid-project assessment is to a) articulate your research problem early on b) encourage conceptual organisation c) promote professional reflection on complex issues around supervision, communication and project management d) foster the use of ubiquitous project management tools such as mind maps and GANTT charts and e) enable responsible use of generative AI.

N.B. You will not be penalised if your research strategy changes after this assessment piece is submitted. Indeed, adaptation, pivots and change are acknowledged as a normal and healthy part of research science. This is intended to be a constructive process – not a punitive one.

The assessment will a) introduce you to tools and processes that help scientists meet deadlines without undue stress and time pressure but also b) support productive change, should the evidence indicate to you and your team that change is required.

You are given 4 tasks to complete:

·      Proposal / strategy document (1 page)

·      Mind map (1 page)

·      GANTT chart (1 page)

You are encouraged to develop these in consultation with your project supervisor WITHOUT recourse to AI.

Once you have mature drafts of all three in place you are then encouraged to use a generative AI system of your choice for provision of feedback and suggestions for improvement.

The assessment document available on BB includes some links to supporting Freeware.

Acknowledging and/or referencing AI use 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference and acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to reference and acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.  

The use of AI or MT must be referenced and acknowledged appropriately. The use of AI and / or MT must be acknowledged using a statement recorded on the coversheet. The statement must contain a description of the AI system that has been engaged and detail around the nature of use. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use. 

Component 2 - Reflection (Complete/Incomplete hurdle)

You will complete a professional reflection and questionnaire (there are 13 questions to guide your reflection). This will be reviewed by the course coordinator.

As part of your professional reflection, you will then be asked to make specific comments on the utility of generative AI in the context of these 3 activities.

You are encouraged to develop your personal reflection in consultation with your supervisor. You MUST NOT use generative AI to help you write your personal reflection.

The assessment document available on BB includes further information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit an electronic copy either as a word or pdf file via Turnitin to the relevant submission link in the course Blackboard site by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The research project report is to be presented in the form of a scientific manuscript potentially suitable for publication in a professional journal.

The exact length and structure of your report may vary across disciplines – please seek specific advice from your supervisor regarding your interpretation of these guidelines.

Broadly speaking, we anticipate the report consisting of a title, abstract (< 300 words), introduction / background / literature review (< 600 words for science reports, but a much longer, more comprehensive literature review for social science and agribusiness reports), methodology including statistical analysis (< 2000 words), results (< 2000 words), discussion (< 1500 words), conclusions (< 200 words) and references.

In general concision is encouraged, the upper word count limits are to be considered guidelines only and represent a conversation starter for discussion with your supervisor.

Two independent examiners who specialise in your area of research will mark your work. The average of your marks from both markers will be used as your grade. In the event that there is a difference of more than 10 points between markers your report will be sent to an independent marker and a decision made which may involve taking the average of the 3 marks.

This assessment item is not eligible for a re-mark as it has been independently marked by two examiners and has undergone the prescribed moderation and review processes. As a result, there is no further academic judgement to be reconsidered through a re-mark process.

Students who believe an administrative error has occurred (for example, an error in mark calculation, data entry, or grade recording) may submit a query to the course coordinator for review. Any identified administrative errors will be corrected where appropriate.

Acknowledging and/or referencing AI use 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference and acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to reference and acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.  

The use of AI or MT must be referenced and acknowledged appropriately. The use of AI and / or MT must be acknowledged using a statement recorded on the coversheet. The statement must contain a description of the AI system that has been engaged and detail around the nature of use. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use. 

Marking timeframes are expected to be at minimum two weeks, for independent markers to review your work fairly and comprehensively. For students with approved extensions on this assessment item, please note that this may delay graduation, while marking is delayed.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students should submit either a word or pdf file via Turnitin on the course Blackboard site, via the relevant submission links by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit. Students will need to submit their Report twice (the same version) because there will be TWO (2) examiners. Therefore, please allow sufficient time on the due date to submit it twice (two submission links will be available).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
24/05/2027 - 28/05/2027

Seminars will occur over the 2-days as indicated in the timetable. Should student numbers be greater than the scheduled time, an additional day may be scheduled into the timetable, following confirmation of total number of students enrolled in the AGRC-code research project courses.
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to give a 5 minute live 'in person' oral presentation at a student conference in Week 13. Presentations will be marked by two examiners, with the averages of these two marks to be used as the final assessment mark.

There will be 3 minutes allocated for questions.

Students should use appropriate visual aids (e.g. PowerPoint Slides) based on the UQ template to assist your presentation. 

Marks will be deducted for presentations that go over the allotted time.

Presentation marking criteria, plus an example of presentation slides and a presentation template are available in the course Blackboard site. 

This assessment item is not eligible for a re-mark as it has been independently marked by two examiners and has undergone the prescribed moderation and review processes. As a result, there is no further academic judgement to be reconsidered through a re-mark process.

Students who believe an administrative error has occurred (for example, an error in mark calculation, data entry, or grade recording) may submit a query to the course coordinator for review. Any identified administrative errors will be corrected where appropriate.

Presentation component

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference and acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to reference and acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.  

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. 

The use of AI and / or MT must be acknowledged using a statement recorded on one of your Powerpoint (or similar) slides. The statement must contain a description of the AI system that has been engaged and detail around the nature of use. Please see assessment guidelines in the blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use. 

Q&A component

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students will deliver a live 5 minute oral presentation in a conference style setting. Some time will be set aside for questions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Performance as determined by project supervisor
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Project
assessments.task_description:
Your academic supervisor will assess your overall performance through the life cycle of your research project.

'Performance' here includes the indicators below that your supervisor deems of relevance to your circumstances:

1) Evidence of planning & time management.

2) Evidence of punctuality.

3) Evidence of resourcefulness.

4) Evidence of independence.

5) Evidence of creativity.

6) Evidence of 'managing up' well.

7) Evidence of determination, and other positive temperamental characteristics, in overcoming challenges.

8) Evidence of strong inter-personal communication.

9) Evidence of integrity and ethical awareness.

10) Evidence for record keeping

Students are not required to submit this assessment item. AGFS Admin will contact your principal supervisor directly with the marking rubric, and will work with them to have the assessment item completed and returned.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This submission is completed by your Supervisor, and the Supervisor will be responsible for submitting this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Students will not receive late penalties if their Supervisor submits this assessment past the due date and time. The Course staff will follow up with relevant Supervisors.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

Independent Project

This course is based on independent study under the supervision of an academic staff member.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Research Project Writing Workshops

The School provides students a series of optional academic and project writing workshops throughout the semester, when able. Students are strongly encouraged to attend these sessions and/or other writing support options, which are designed to assist in research project writing.


Sub-activity: Research Project Writing Workshop Series or Recordings - Suresh Krishnasamy

Learning outcomes: L03
